title: betula alba var. vulgaris
intro: Betula alba var. vulgaris, also known as white birch, is a variety of the birch tree (Betula alba) that is native to Europe and Asia. The bark of this plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of white birch include relieving symptoms of arthritis, reducing inflammation, and soothing skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of white birch bark, including teas and tinctures, can be used to support overall health and well-being.
